channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Man charged in NYC subway chokehold death set to stand trial
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — To some New Yorkers, he’s the white vigilante who choked an innocent Black man to death on the subway. To others, he’s the U.S. Marine Corps veteran whose attempt to subdue a mentally ill man ended in tragedy.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Singer named as victim in couple's alleged Las Vegas theft spree
item.description: A married couple allegedly stole hundreds of thousands of dollars worth of valuables as Las Vegas hosted the Super Bowl -- with one theft involving a popular singer, according to documents the 8 News Now Investigators obtained.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, NewsNation

item.title: WWE legends Undertaker and Kane endorse Donald Trump for President in TikTok video
item.description: (KRON) -- In a video posted Friday on Donald Trump’s official TikTok account, titled "America Depends on It," legendary wrestlers The Undertaker and Kane throw their support behind Trump for President, calling out his Election Day opponent with a wrestling challenge.
item.dc:creator: Tor Smith, KRON4

item.title: Iowa school shooter ‘likely displayed warning signs’ before January attack: Report
item.description: The shooter responsible for two deaths and multiple injuries at an Iowa high school earlier this year “likely displayed warning signs” according to a detailed report from state officials released Friday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Utah court allows Australian student to play football after lawsuit filed
item.description: An international student who filed a discrimination lawsuit earlier this month has been allowed to play on his high school's football team.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, NewsNation

item.title: Child labor violations on the rise, problem could get worse: report
item.description: Between 2011 and 2020, the rate of injuries among workers under 18 nearly doubled, a recent report says.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, KRON4

item.title: Santa Ana winds ignite red flag warnings, fire concerns for Southern California
item.description: Gusty Santa Ana winds and warmer temperatures have officials concerned about an increased chance of wildfires erupting in Southern California heading into the weekend. Red flag warnings have been issued for most mountain and valley locations of Los Angeles and Ventura counties from 9 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, NewsNation

item.title: 2 people shown on camera leaving fatally shot teen at Las Vegas hospital
item.description: North Las Vegas police are asking for the public's help to identify two people they say were caught on camera leaving a fatally shot teen in a hospital lobby.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Overton, KRON4

item.title: Singer Zayn postpones US tour after the death of his former bandmate Liam Payne
item.description: English singer Zayn Malik on Saturday postponed a planned tour to the U.S. following the death of his former One Direction bandmate Liam Payne.
item.dc:creator: NICK PERRY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Child labor violations on the rise, problem could get worse: report
item.description: Between 2011 and 2020, the rate of injuries among workers under 18 nearly doubled, a recent report says.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Utah court allows Australian student to play high school football after lawsuit filed
item.description: An international student who filed a discrimination lawsuit earlier this month has been allowed to play on his high school's football team.
item.dc:creator: Aubree B. Jennings, WGN 9

item.title: NYC officials envision turning Fifth Avenue into a grand boulevard
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Manhattan’s famed luxury store row Fifth Avenue is in line for a major makeover. New York City officials unveiled a plan this week to transform a central portion of the thoroughfare between Bryant Park and Central Park into a more pedestrian-centered boulevard.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hurricane Oscar makes landfall in the Bahamas and heads toward Cuba
item.description: Hurricane Oscar made landfall in the southeastern Bahamas and headed toward Cuba, an island beleaguered by a massive power outage.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Where is the 'best restroom' in the U.S.? In a Utah gas station
item.description: The runners-up of this year's contest included a disco party bathroom, a bathroom full of mirrors and neon lights, and a very reflective 80s-inspired restroom in a brunch restaurant, among others.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, Fox 5

item.title: Stanford psychologist behind the controversial "Stanford Prison Experiment" dies at 91
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Philip G. Zimbardo, the psychologist behind the controversial “Stanford Prison Experiment” that was intended to examine the psychological experiences of imprisonment, has died. He was 91. Stanford University announced Friday that Zimbardo died Oct.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Two Texas cities represent the divide between those who vote and those who could, but often don't
item.description: LEWISVILLE, Texas (AP) — Deep in the heart of Texas' sprawl, the city of Lewisville embodies the Lone Star State.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Philadelphia man fatally stabbed inside laundromat for 'no apparent reason': police
item.description: A Philadelphia man was stabbed and killed while playing a game inside a laundromat in an attack police say was carried out for "no apparent reason.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Menendez brothers’ former Beverly Hills mansion attracts onlookers, 911 calls as killers seek freedom: police
item.description: A recent series on the Menendez brothers' case has sparked an uptick in 911 calls made to police concerning the notorious Beverly Hills mansion where double-murder took place.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California man allegedly murdered roommate, buried body in backyard: police
item.description: A California man was arrested after allegedly killing his roommate and burying the body in his backyard following an altercation with the victim.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Yankees get to Guardians closer Clase again, score 2 runs in 9th to win 8-6 and take 3-1 ALCS lead
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Cleveland All-Star closer Emmanuel Clase gave up two runs in the ninth inning and the New York Yankees moved closer to another World Series, beating the Guardians 8-6 on Friday night to take a 3-1 lead in the American League Championship Series.
item.dc:creator: TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The last in-person vote in the US will be cast on the desolate tundra of Alaska's Aleutian Islands
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — On a desolate slab of island tundra in western Alaska, a resident of Adak will again become the last American to cast an in-person ballot for president, continuing a 12-year tradition for the nation’s westernmost community.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Crews halt fast-moving brush fire that burned 2 Oakland homes, forced 500 evacuations
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A fast-moving fire fed by strong winds burned two homes Friday and damaged several others in a hillside neighborhood in the city of Oakland, where hundreds of people were ordered to evacuate before more than 100 firefighters stopped the progress of the flames, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Alonso and Mets extend season again, beating Dodgers 12-6 to send NLCS back to LA for Game 6
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Not easy to knock out these New York Mets. Pete Alonso golfed an early three-run homer and the Mets hammered an ineffective Jack Flaherty, extending the National League Championship Series with a 12-6 victory over the Los Angeles Dodgers in Game 5 on Friday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE FITZPATRICK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump microphone cuts out for 20 minutes at Detroit rally
item.description: Trump was in the midst of speaking about his tariff policy at the event on Friday, about to call it “the most beautiful word in the dictionary,” before the microphone went down. Someone handed the former president another microphone to try, but it didn’t work either.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, ABC 4

item.title: Iowa school shooter ‘likely displayed warning signs’ before January attack, report finds
item.description: The shooter responsible for two deaths and multiple injuries at an Iowa high school earlier this year “likely displayed warning signs” according to a detailed report from state officials released Friday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: California woman slashed dog's throat, left it alive in trash: Sacramento deputies
item.description: The woman "felt her life would be easier if she only had to care for one dog and cut one of the dog’s throats," say officials.
item.dc:creator: Veronica Catlin, WGN 9

item.title: Indiana prosecutors say unspent bullet, video provide proof of man's guilt in horrific Delphi murders
item.description: A man accused of brutally killing two young girls on an Indiana hiking trail in 2017 is standing trial in the murders seven years later.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Wisconsin US Senate candidates trade accusations of lying during testy debate
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Democratic U.S. Sen. Tammy Baldwin and Republican challenger Eric Hovde repeatedly accused each other of lying over the course of their closely watched Senate race during an often-testy and confrontational debate Friday.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Liam Payne’s 1D bandmates, Cheryl, Simon Cowell and more mourn singer
item.description: Friends, collaborators and fellow musicians have expressed shock and sadness over the death of former One Direction singer Liam Payne, who died in Buenos Aires, Argentina, on Wednesday at age 31.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Hundreds ordered to evacuate from fast-moving Northern California fire, official says
item.description: At least seven homes were burned by a fast-moving fire in a hillside neighborhood of Oakland, California; hundreds of people were ordered to evacuate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Montana authorities searching for killer as death reported as bear attack now investigated as 'brutal' murder
item.description: After a friend reported that Dustin Kjersem may have died from a bear attack last Saturday, Montana authorities confirmed he was "brutally" murdered, but have yet to find a suspect.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kentucky family demands answers after organs nearly taken from living man
item.description: A Kentucky family is looking for answers after a man was declared brain dead and being prepared to have his organs harvested while he was still alive.
item.dc:creator: Kiley Hill, KRON4

item.title: Grandma who texted stranger for Thanksgiving announces breast cancer diagnosis
item.description: The Arizona grandmother who sent a text message to what she believed was her grandson’s phone number, inviting him over for Thanksgiving dinner nearly nine years ago, has announced her diagnosis of breast cancer.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, WGN 9

item.title: Universal Orlando announces Epic Universe opening date
item.description: The theme park will feature more than 50 attractions, as well as entertainment, dining and shopping options across five “expansive worlds.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, KTLA 5

item.title: Daughter claims mother's body 'misplaced' in Memphis cemetery
item.description: "They have no idea where or how she was lost, but they do not know where she is," says Georleshia Minter.
item.dc:creator: Quametra Wilborn, Fox 5

item.title: Electoral battleground North Carolina starts early in-person voting while recovering from Helene
item.description: ASHEVILLE, N.C. (AP) — Early in-person voting began statewide Thursday in the presidential battleground of North Carolina, including in mountainous areas where thousands of potential voters still lack power and clean running water after Hurricane Helene's epic flooding.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden delivers final eulogy during Ethel Kennedy memorial service in DC
item.description: Ethel Kennedy, 96, died last week. She had been hospitalized after suffering a stroke earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9
